No history snapshot available. I'll proceed with the breakthrough idea directly. The key structural change: suppression multiplies **only** the elongation channel, not the whole rate.

The current best form applies suppression to the whole rate (seed + primary + elongation). The breakthrough separates the channels: at high inhibitor dose the c-dependent autocatalytic burst is throttled, but the c-independent nucleation floor (seed + primary) still drives the system to plateau. This changes the slope profile qualitatively — stretching the lag and shifting half-time in a dose-dependent way that a uniform multiplicative slowdown cannot produce.

This is physically grounded: Brichos-type and secondary-nucleation inhibitors bind fibril surfaces and preferentially shut down the surface-catalyzed autocatalytic channel, leaving primary/seed initiation largely intact.

**Research summary:** External ML paper search returned only unrelated binder-design papers; web search timed out; the local JBC reference file is empty. So I rely on the well-established Cohen/Knowles amyloid master-equation framework (Cohen et al. PNAS 2013; Arosio, Michaels et al.). The key documented principle is that different inhibitor classes act on **distinct microscopic steps**: Brichos-type chaperones and surface-binding molecules preferentially suppress the **surface-catalyzed secondary/autocatalytic (c-dependent) channel**, while primary nucleation and elongation of existing ends respond differently. Step-selective inhibition changes the *shape/timing* of the sigmoid (lag stretching, half-time shift) rather than uniformly rescaling the rate — precisely the shape-loss signature I aim to reduce.

ultiplies **only** `elongation` (the `monomer*concentration`, autocatalytic term), giving
`d(c)/dt = (P − c)·[seed_nuc + primary_nuc + suppression·elongation]`.
This decouples a throttleable fast burst from an unthrottled slow floor, which is the mechanism most likely to reduce the dominant shape loss (0.088) while holding NMSE near current. Same 6 squared constants, same 1e6 scaling, bounded denominator ≥ 1, exact cd=0 limit — single straight-line `evaluate_expression` call, contract preserved.
